restaurant-review-management-software.title: Restaurant Review Management Software | ProInsights
restaurant-review-management-software.description: Daily Google + TripAdvisor review intelligence for restaurants. AI summaries, 1-star alerts, named-dish and staff mentions, competitor tracking, AI-drafted replies.
restaurant-review-management-software.h1: Restaurant review management software built for daily action
restaurant-review-management-software.intro: One short email at 8am. One WhatsApp message you read between covers. Every new review across Google and TripAdvisor, summarised by Claude AI, with the dishes that landed well, the staff who got named, and any one-star review you need to act on before service. Built for restaurant operators in Cyprus and across Europe who do not want to learn another dashboard.
restaurant-review-management-software.body_html:
DAILY MONITORING

Every new Google and TripAdvisor review, before service.

ProInsights checks your Google Business Profile and TripAdvisor listing every night and assembles a single morning report. New reviews are read by AI, scored for sentiment, tagged with themes, and ranked by what actually needs your attention. The format is an email and an optional WhatsApp message — not a dashboard you have to remember to open.

  • New reviews across Google Maps and TripAdvisor, daily
  • Star rating delta versus yesterday, last week, last month
  • Themes auto-detected (service, food, price, ambience, noise, wait time)
  • Sentiment per review, in plain English

DISH, STAFF, SERVICE MENTIONS

Find out which dishes guests are talking about — and which staff get named.

Restaurants are not hotels. The signal hides in named dishes and named team members. ProInsights surfaces both, automatically.

Dish leaderboard

The dishes mentioned most in the last 7, 30 and 90 days. Praised dishes ranked separately from criticised ones. Useful when you are deciding what to push, what to retire, and what the kitchen needs to fix.

Staff mentions

Named team members are extracted and counted. You see who is being thanked publicly and who is being singled out in complaints. Hand them the printout at the next briefing.

Service categories

Welcome, pace, attentiveness, knowledge, billing, and farewell — each tracked as its own theme so you can see where the friction is, not just that it exists.

1-STAR ALERT

A one-star review should not wait until the morning email.

On Pro and above, any new one-star review triggers an instant WhatsApp and email alert within 90 minutes of appearing, during your operating hours. The alert includes the full review text, the platform, the timestamp, and a flag if the review mentions food safety, allergy, hygiene or a legal threat — sensitive cases are escalated and never auto-replied.

This is the difference between hearing about a bad review from a guest on social media versus catching it before lunch service. It is the single most-used feature for restaurants that take reputation seriously.

COMPETITOR TRACKING

Five to ten named competitors, watched daily.

Name your direct competitors at sign-up. ProInsights tracks their new reviews and ratings every day, so you see what they are getting praised for, what they are getting criticised for, and how your rating compares week over week.

Advanced tier tracks five competitors. Pro tier tracks ten. You get a weekly side-by-side that fits on one screen — average rating, review volume, themes trending up, themes trending down. No data extracts to download, no formulas to maintain.

AI REPLY DRAFTS

Diplomatic replies, drafted in your house voice.

On Pro, every new review can come back with a draft reply written in the tone you set during onboarding — warm, formal, regional, multilingual. You approve, edit, or rewrite. ProInsights never publishes without you.

Replies that touch food safety, allergy, hygiene or any legal threat are deliberately not auto-drafted. Those go to a human escalation queue. We do not hand the keys to AI for content that can hurt your business.

DASHBOARD AND ARCHIVE

A web dashboard you do not have to live in.

The dashboard is there when you want it — every daily, weekly and monthly report archived, searchable, and exportable. Filter by date range. Pull staff mention counts for a performance review. Send a board pack as a PDF. But the dashboard is the backup, not the workflow. The email and WhatsApp are the workflow.

START

See what your reviews are telling you.

Twenty-minute demo. We will generate a personalised sample for your restaurant during the call. First real report in your inbox within 24 hours of signature.

Book a demo

Related: Google review monitoring for restaurants · AI reply generator · Pricing

faq.q: Which platforms do you monitor for restaurants?
faq.a: Google Maps and TripAdvisor. Those are the two platforms that actually move bookings for independent restaurants in Europe. We do not pretend to monitor sources we cannot reliably read.

faq.q: How quickly will I get my first report?
faq.a: Within 24 hours of signing the order form. Onboarding is a 15-minute call where you hand us your Google Business Profile URL and TripAdvisor URL, name your competitors, and pick the tone of voice for AI replies.

faq.q: Will the AI ever reply to a review without me seeing it first?
faq.a: No. ProInsights drafts replies. You approve or edit them. We never publish on your behalf. Reviews touching food safety, allergy or legal threats are explicitly not auto-drafted at all — they are flagged for human review.

faq.q: Can I track dishes by exact name?
faq.a: Yes. Named dishes are extracted automatically from review text and grouped by mention count, sentiment, and trend. You can also add your menu items at onboarding so we match informal mentions to the canonical name.

faq.q: How much does it cost for restaurants?
faq.a: Restaurant pricing is tailored to your venue size and the number of competitors you want tracked. Email hello@proinsights.com.cy and we will quote within one working day.

faq.q: What happens if I am not getting value after a month?
faq.a: Cancel within 30 days of going live and we refund the subscription, no questions asked. No setup fee, no exit fee, no contract trap.

hotel-reputation-management-software.title: Hotel Reputation Management Software | ProInsights
hotel-reputation-management-software.description: Daily hotel review intelligence across Google, TripAdvisor, Booking.com, Expedia and Trip.com. Sentiment by department, verified rate comparison, competitor benchmarking.
hotel-reputation-management-software.h1: Hotel reputation management software for operators, not enterprises
hotel-reputation-management-software.intro: A daily briefing your GM can read with coffee. Every new guest review across Google, TripAdvisor, Booking.com, Expedia, Trip.com, ZenHotels and HolidayCheck — read by AI, sorted by department, benchmarked against your five to ten direct competitors. Built in Cyprus for independent hotels and boutique groups that want intelligence without an enterprise rollout.
hotel-reputation-management-software.body_html:
GUEST REVIEW MONITORING

Seven sources, one report.

ProInsights pulls new guest reviews every night from the channels that matter to European hotels and consolidates them into one morning briefing. No tab switching, no dashboard fatigue, no Monday-morning scramble.

  • Google — Business Profile reviews and rating
  • TripAdvisor — guest reviews, ranking, traveller photos
  • Booking.com — guest reviews
  • Expedia — guest reviews
  • Trip.com — guest reviews
  • ZenHotels — guest reviews
  • HolidayCheck — guest reviews

Each review is scored for sentiment, tagged with themes, and routed to the right department in the report. Nothing else is claimed. If a source is not on this list, we do not pretend to read it.

SENTIMENT BY DEPARTMENT

Read the hotel the way you run it.

Reviews are sliced by the categories your team owns, so the right line manager sees the right signal.

Front of house

Check-in, check-out, concierge, F&B service, named staff. The signal a front-office manager actually needs.

Housekeeping

Cleanliness, room condition, linen, bathroom, smell. Trend lines per week and per month so you can spot drift early.

Breakfast and F&B

Breakfast variety, quality, opening times. Restaurant and bar mentioned separately. Praised and criticised dishes called out by name.

Room product

Comfort, size, bed, soundproofing, AC, view. The structural complaints that capex needs to see, not the ops team.

Location

Walkability, transport, distance to beach, distance to centre. Useful when you are positioning against properties with a different geography.

Value and price

Whether guests felt the rate matched the experience. Cross-referenced with your verified rate position against competitors.

COMPETITOR BENCHMARKING

Five to ten named competitors, watched every day.

You pick your competitors at sign-up. We track their reviews across the same seven sources, daily, and surface the side-by-side: average rating, review volume, themes trending up at their property, themes trending down at yours.

Expert tier adds a weekly Strengths and Weaknesses analysis — what each competitor is being praised for, what each is being criticised for, in plain English. The quarterly deep-dive briefing maps your competitive position over time so you can see drift before it shows in your rate.

VERIFIED RATE COMPARISON

Rates with provenance — or no rate at all.

Pro and Expert tiers include rate comparison against your competitor set. Every rate we display is verified with source, timestamp, occupancy, currency, and a confidence status. Rates are hidden when data quality is not safe to display. We do not show estimates dressed up as facts.

Expert tier extends this into a room-rate market analysis: room-category match, demand-day pricing patterns, and parity-violation flags across up to six competitors. The aim is to give your revenue manager a defensible position to work from — not a number nobody can stand behind.

DAILY MANAGER REPORT

One email. Three lines at the top. Everything else below.

The morning report opens with a three-line AI brief — what changed overnight, what needs action today, what to watch this week. Then the detail: new reviews by source, department sentiment shifts, theme movement, competitor moves, and any one-star review that demands an urgent alert response.

On Pro, the same brief lands on your phone as a WhatsApp message. One phone is included on Pro; additional phones are €10 a month each. Unlimited email recipients on Pro and above so your DOM, front office manager and ownership all see the same thing at the same time.

DASHBOARD

A web dashboard you can ignore most of the time.

Every report you have ever received is archived, searchable, and exportable as PDF. Filter by department, by source, by date range. Pull a board pack in two clicks. But the dashboard is not the workflow — the email and the WhatsApp are the workflow. The dashboard is for the deeper look on Friday afternoon.

START

See your hotel through your guests' eyes.

Twenty-minute demo. We generate a personalised sample for your property during the call. First real report within 24 hours of signature. Cancel within 30 days for a full refund.

Book a demo

Related: TripAdvisor monitoring · Competitor benchmarking · ReviewPro alternative · Pricing

faq.q: Which review sources do you monitor for hotels?
faq.a: Seven: Google, TripAdvisor, Booking.com, Expedia, Trip.com, ZenHotels and HolidayCheck. That is the full list. If a source matters to you and is not listed, ask us — we will be honest about whether it is coming or not.

faq.q: How is your rate comparison different from a rate-shopper tool?
faq.a: We are not a rate shopper. We display rates we have verified with source, timestamp, occupancy, currency and a confidence status, and we hide them when data quality is not safe to display. The aim is intelligence you can defend in a revenue meeting, not a daily number that may or may not be real.

faq.q: How does ProInsights compare to Shiji ReviewPro?
faq.a: ReviewPro is a powerful enterprise platform built for global chains with central reputation teams. ProInsights is built for independent hotels and boutique groups that want a daily briefing on email and WhatsApp without an enterprise rollout. See the side-by-side on our ReviewPro alternative page.

faq.q: Can our DOM, front office manager and ownership all receive the report?
faq.a: Yes. Advanced tier supports up to 5 recipients. Pro and Expert support unlimited recipients. One WhatsApp phone is included on Pro and above; extra phones are €10 a month each.

faq.q: What is the 1-star alert?
faq.a: On Pro and above, any new one-star review across your monitored sources triggers a WhatsApp and email alert within 90 minutes during operating hours. Reviews mentioning food safety, allergy, hygiene or legal threats are auto-escalated and never auto-replied.

faq.q: What does pricing look like for hotels?
faq.a: Basic €79, Advanced €139, Pro €189, Expert €249 per month per property. Multi-property: 20% off the second and subsequent properties. Full breakdown on our pricing page.

google-review-monitoring-restaurants.title: Google Review Monitoring for Restaurants | ProInsights
google-review-monitoring-restaurants.description: Daily Google review monitoring for restaurants. AI summaries, instant 1-star alerts, named dish and staff mentions, AI-drafted replies, competitor tracking.
google-review-monitoring-restaurants.h1: Google review monitoring for restaurants — every morning, in your inbox
google-review-monitoring-restaurants.intro: Google reviews move covers more than any other platform for independent restaurants. ProInsights reads every new Google review for your restaurant overnight, scores it, tags it, ranks it, and delivers a single short email at 8am — plus an optional WhatsApp message and an urgent alert within 90 minutes on any one-star review.
google-review-monitoring-restaurants.body_html:
WHY GOOGLE FIRST

Google is where the booking starts.

For most independent restaurants in Europe, a guest decides whether to book within ten seconds of seeing your Google Business Profile. The star rating, the most recent reviews, and how you respond to them shape that decision. If you do not have a system that reads every new Google review on the day it lands, you are managing your reputation in arrears.

ProInsights is built around that one fact. Google Maps is a first-class source — not bolted on as an afterthought, not lumped into a generic crawler. We read Google reviews as they appear, sort them by sentiment, and tell you in three lines what changed overnight.

The platform is built specifically for restaurants that do not have a marketing department in the back office. The morning email is short enough that a busy owner-operator can read it before service prep, but detailed enough that the head chef and the floor manager can act on it together. We have spent a lot of time deciding what to leave out, not just what to put in.

DAILY BRIEF

What you get every morning at 8am.

The three-line summary

What changed overnight, what to act on today, what to watch this week. Written by Claude AI in plain English. No charts to interpret, no jargon, no marketing language.

Every new Google review

Full text, star rating, sentiment, themes, named dishes, named staff. Sorted so the urgent ones are at the top of the email, not buried at the bottom.

Rating delta

Your current Google star rating versus yesterday, last week, last month. The numbers your reservations team should know without being asked.

1-STAR ALERT

When a one-star review lands, you hear about it fast.

On Pro tier and above, any new one-star Google review triggers a WhatsApp and email alert within 90 minutes during operating hours. The alert includes the full review text and a flag if the review mentions food safety, allergy, hygiene or a legal threat. Those reviews are routed to a human escalation queue — never auto-replied.

This is the feature most operators say they wish they had during the last bad week. Catching a one-star at 12:15 versus 16:45 is the difference between a recovered guest and a public argument. A reply written calmly within an hour reads completely differently from one written defensively two days later.

AI REPLY DRAFTS

A draft reply, in your voice, ready when you are.

On Pro, every new Google review can come with a draft reply written in the tone you set at onboarding. Warm or formal. English, Greek, French, German, Italian, Spanish, or any language the original review was written in. You approve, edit, or rewrite. ProInsights does not publish without your sign-off.

The replies are not generic. The tone profile we build at onboarding makes a measurable difference — a Greek-village taverna does not sound like a Michelin-listed restaurant, and the drafts reflect that. You can also keep house phrases and ban corporate clichés, so the voice stays yours across hundreds of replies.

DISHES AND STAFF

Find the dishes and team members being talked about.

Google reviews are full of specifics: a server's name, a dish, a wait time, a corkage charge. ProInsights extracts those automatically and ranks them. You see which dishes are getting praised, which are getting criticised, and which team members are being singled out by name — positively or negatively. Useful for the next pre-shift briefing.

Praised dishes are listed separately from criticised dishes, so the same dish can appear in both lists if guests are split on it. The named-staff section is one many managers print and bring to weekly briefings — it makes it easy to thank a server publicly and to have an honest conversation with one who keeps getting flagged for pace.

START

Stop checking Google manually.

Twenty-minute demo, personalised sample on the call, first report inside 24 hours of signature.

Book a demo

Related: Restaurant review management · AI reply generator · Pricing

faq.q: Do I need a Google API key or special access?
faq.a: No. We use your public Google Business Profile URL. You hand us the link, we do the rest.

faq.q: How quickly does a new Google review show up in my report?
faq.a: New reviews are pulled overnight and appear in the next morning's report. One-star reviews on Pro and above also trigger an instant alert within 90 minutes during operating hours.

faq.q: Can the AI reply to Google reviews on my behalf?
faq.a: ProInsights drafts replies. You approve or edit them before they go live. We never publish without your sign-off, and we never draft replies to reviews mentioning food safety, allergy or legal threats — those are human-only.

faq.q: Will this work for a restaurant with very few reviews?
faq.a: Yes. The daily report is just as useful for a restaurant getting two reviews a week as one getting twenty. With low volume, the trend lines become more important than the daily count.

faq.q: Does this also monitor TripAdvisor?
faq.a: Yes — restaurants on ProInsights are monitored on both Google Maps and TripAdvisor as standard.

tripadvisor-review-monitoring-hotels.title: TripAdvisor Review Monitoring for Hotels | ProInsights
tripadvisor-review-monitoring-hotels.description: Daily TripAdvisor review monitoring for hotels. Ranking shifts, department sentiment, named-staff mentions, competitor tracking and urgent 1-star alerts.
tripadvisor-review-monitoring-hotels.h1: TripAdvisor review monitoring for hotels — built around how the ranking actually moves
tripadvisor-review-monitoring-hotels.intro: TripAdvisor still drives bookings, especially for independent hotels and boutique groups in Mediterranean Europe. ProInsights reads every new TripAdvisor review for your property overnight, watches your ranking, surfaces sentiment by department, and benchmarks you against the competitor set you choose.
tripadvisor-review-monitoring-hotels.body_html:
DAILY TRIPADVISOR MONITORING

Every new review, every morning, no logging in.

Each night ProInsights checks your TripAdvisor listing for new reviews and rating changes. By 8am you have an email summary covering what was written, the sentiment of each new review, the themes they touched, and any movement on your overall rating.

The report is built so the GM can read it in two minutes and the DOM can read it for ten. Nothing is buried, nothing is padded. Reviews are listed with the most consequential first — a one-bubble review with a food-safety complaint comes above a five-bubble review praising the breakfast spread, because that is the order in which you need to act on them.

The format is the same whether you receive two new reviews a week or twenty a day. We have built it around the principle that you should never have to scan through padding to find the thing that matters.

SENTIMENT BY DEPARTMENT

Service. Cleanliness. Value. Location. Room. Sleep quality.

TripAdvisor reviews are categorised against the dimensions hotel operators actually run their teams against. Each new review is routed to the right department in the report, with a sentiment score and named-staff mentions where they appear.

You can see at a glance whether the housekeeping trend is improving, whether breakfast complaints have spiked, and whether a named team member is collecting public thanks — the kind of intelligence that should drive your weekly ops meeting. The DOM gets the commercial picture, the front-office manager gets the service picture, and the head of housekeeping gets a clean view of every cleanliness mention in the last seven days without having to read every full review.

RANKING WATCH

Know when your ranking moves, not after.

TripAdvisor ranking shifts quietly. ProInsights tracks your position in your destination's hotel list and flags meaningful changes — both up and down. You see the movement in context: the ranking, the rating, and the review volume that drove it.

A small ranking gain over a quiet week is not the same story as a large drop following a critical-review cluster. We do not just report the number — we set it next to the cause, so the morning brief gives you a defensible explanation for ownership.

COMPETITOR BENCHMARKING

Five to ten competitors, watched the same way.

You name your direct competitors at sign-up. ProInsights tracks their TripAdvisor reviews and ratings every day on the same cadence as yours. The weekly side-by-side covers average rating, review volume, themes trending up at their property, themes trending down at yours.

Expert tier extends this with a strengths and weaknesses analysis per competitor, written in plain English. The aim is intelligence your commercial team can act on, not a chart that needs interpreting. If a peer property has had a sudden cluster of cleanliness complaints, your DOM should be able to read that fact in one sentence and decide what to do with it.

1-STAR ALERT

A bad review needs you within thirty minutes, not tomorrow.

On Pro and above, any new one-star TripAdvisor review (or one-bubble in TripAdvisor's terms) triggers a WhatsApp and email alert within 90 minutes during operating hours. Reviews mentioning sensitive topics — illness, injury, allergy, legal threat — are flagged for human handling and never auto-replied. You get the alert with full context and a starting position for a reply, but the publication is always under your control.

START

Watch your TripAdvisor the way an operator should.

Twenty-minute demo with a personalised sample for your property. First real report inside 24 hours of signature.

Book a demo

Related: Hotel reputation management · Competitor benchmarking · Pricing

faq.q: Do you watch TripAdvisor ranking, not just reviews?
faq.a: Yes. We track your position in your destination's hotel ranking and flag meaningful movements. The morning report shows ranking alongside rating and review volume so changes have context.

faq.q: Can I track restaurant-style TripAdvisor listings as well?
faq.a: Yes — TripAdvisor is a standard source for both our hotel and restaurant verticals. Restaurants on TripAdvisor are monitored exactly the same way as hotels.

faq.q: How is sentiment scored?
faq.a: Every review is read by Claude AI and scored on sentiment per theme, not just overall. A review that praises the room but criticises breakfast will register both signals correctly.

faq.q: What about TripAdvisor sub-scores like service and location?
faq.a: We do not read TripAdvisor's own category sub-ratings today. Instead, the text of every review is analysed and scored by department (service, food, cleanliness, value, ambience, location), which gives you the same category-level trend view across all your sources, not just TripAdvisor.

faq.q: Do you draft replies to TripAdvisor reviews?
faq.a: On Pro and above, yes — diplomatic drafts in your house voice, in the language the review was written in. You approve before anything goes live. Reviews touching sensitive topics are routed to humans only.

ai-review-response-generator-restaurants.title: AI Review Response Generator for Restaurants | ProInsights
ai-review-response-generator-restaurants.description: AI-drafted review replies for restaurants. Brand-voice tuned, multilingual, with smart snippets. You approve every reply — ProInsights never publishes without you.
ai-review-response-generator-restaurants.h1: AI review response generator for restaurants, with a brand voice you actually set
ai-review-response-generator-restaurants.intro: Reply to every new Google and TripAdvisor review without sounding like a chatbot. ProInsights drafts replies in the tone you choose at onboarding, in the language the review was written in, with smart snippets you reuse and edit. You approve every reply before it goes live. The AI never publishes on its own.
ai-review-response-generator-restaurants.body_html:
HOW IT WORKS

Read review, draft reply, approve, publish.

For every new review that lands on Google or TripAdvisor, ProInsights produces a draft reply that takes the review's content, the venue's tone of voice, and your house policy into account. The draft appears in the morning report and in the dashboard. You can publish it as-is, edit it, or rewrite it entirely.

ProInsights never auto-publishes. The draft is a starting point — not a finished answer. The job of the AI is to do the first 80% of the writing in your voice, so the human review takes thirty seconds instead of fifteen minutes per reply. The remaining 20% — the personal touch, the gesture of recovery, the in-joke with a regular guest — stays with the person who knows the venue.

BRAND VOICE

A house voice you set, not a generic template.

At onboarding, we ask you ten questions about how you want to sound: warm or formal, regional or international, signed by an owner or by a team, ending with an invitation or with a thank-you. The answers become a tone profile that shapes every draft.

Tone

Warm, formal, witty, understated. Pick one or layer two. The same restaurant can use a slightly different tone for praise versus criticism, so good reviews do not all sound like the same form letter.

Signature

Sign every reply with the owner's name, the manager's name, or a team signature. Match what you already use in person and online. You can also configure different signatures for different shifts or different signed-in users.

Phrases to use and avoid

House phrases you always want ("thank you for choosing us") and corporate clichés you never want ("we appreciate your feedback"). Both are honoured. The ban-list is the feature most operators tell us they care most about.

SMART SNIPPETS

Reusable replies for the recurring situations.

Every restaurant has the same five recurring themes: too noisy, too slow on a Saturday, the wine list, the bread, the dessert. ProInsights learns the snippets you use most and offers them when the same theme comes up. You save time on the routine answers, you spend that time on the replies that need real care.

Snippets are not templates. They are short, configurable building blocks that the AI assembles into a draft that still reads like a person wrote it. You can edit any snippet from the dashboard, and the change propagates to every future draft that uses it.

MULTILINGUAL

Reply in the language the review was written in.

A guest leaving a review in French gets a reply in French. A review in Greek gets a reply in Greek. ProInsights detects the review's language and drafts the reply in the same one, keeping your house voice intact across languages.

Currently supported as standard: English, Greek, French, German, Italian, Spanish, Russian, Polish, Dutch and Romanian. Other languages on request. For Cypriot restaurants specifically, the Greek and Russian quality has been tuned with native speakers — reply drafts read naturally rather than as machine translations.

SENSITIVE CONTENT

Some replies are not for AI to draft.

Reviews mentioning food safety, allergy, hygiene, illness, injury, or legal threats are deliberately not auto-drafted. ProInsights flags them, routes them to a separate human-handling queue, and leaves the reply for a senior person on your team. We do not hand the keys to AI for content that can hurt your business in court.

You can add your own custom triggers — a specific dish, a named staff member, a complaint type — that should also route to human-only handling. The triggers are managed from the dashboard and you can change them at any time.

START

Reply like you, only faster.

Twenty-minute demo. We will tune the voice during onboarding so the first drafts already sound like your restaurant.

Book a demo

Related: Restaurant review management · Google review monitoring · Pricing

faq.q: Does the AI ever publish a reply on its own?
faq.a: No. Every reply is drafted, queued, and waits for your approval. ProInsights never publishes to Google or TripAdvisor without an explicit sign-off from your team.

faq.q: What languages do you support?
faq.a: English, Greek, French, German, Italian, Spanish, Russian, Polish, Dutch and Romanian as standard. The draft is generated in whatever language the review was written in. Other languages are available on request.

faq.q: Can I keep the AI from replying to certain types of reviews?
faq.a: Yes. Reviews touching food safety, allergy, illness, injury, hygiene or legal threats are not auto-drafted by default. You can also add custom triggers — for example, any mention of a specific dish or staff member — that should route to human-only handling.

faq.q: Can different team members have different tones?
faq.a: Yes. You can configure one voice for owner-signed replies and another for manager-signed replies. Both are saved as separate tone profiles.

faq.q: Is this included in all tiers?
faq.a: AI-drafted replies are a Pro tier feature for restaurants. On Basic and Advanced you still receive the daily review summary and themes, but not AI reply drafts.

hotel-competitor-benchmarking.title: Hotel Competitor Benchmarking Software | ProInsights
hotel-competitor-benchmarking.description: Daily side-by-side hotel competitor benchmarking across reviews, ratings, sentiment and verified rates. Five to ten named competitors tracked every day.
hotel-competitor-benchmarking.h1: Hotel competitor benchmarking software — five to ten competitors, watched daily
hotel-competitor-benchmarking.intro: Pick the hotels you actually compete with. ProInsights watches their reviews, ratings, sentiment, and verified rates every day, and shows you a side-by-side that fits on one screen. No CSV exports, no formulas, no quarterly dashboards that arrive too late.
hotel-competitor-benchmarking.body_html:
NAMED COMPETITORS

You pick the set. We track it every day.

At sign-up you name five competitors (Advanced) or ten (Pro and Expert). These are real hotels, not algorithmic suggestions. We then monitor each one across the seven review sources we cover: Google, TripAdvisor, Booking.com, Expedia, Trip.com, ZenHotels and HolidayCheck.

Every new review at every tracked competitor is read overnight, sentiment-scored, and theme-tagged. The result lands in your weekly competitor section — and on Expert tier, in the daily competitor review monitoring stream.

You can change your competitor set at any time. If a new resort opens down the road in May and starts taking your shoulder-season demand, swap one of your existing competitors out and add the new one in. Changes apply from the next morning's report.

SIDE-BY-SIDE

One screen, six rows, the comparison your commercial team needs.

The competitor section of your weekly report is a single side-by-side table. For your property and each competitor, you see:

  • Current average rating across each source
  • Review volume in the last 7 and 30 days
  • Top three themes trending up
  • Top three themes trending down
  • Most-mentioned staff and most-mentioned dishes / amenities
  • Verified rate position (Pro and Expert, hotel only)

Nothing in there that needs a quant to interpret. Nothing missing that a GM would have to chase. The table is exportable to PDF in two clicks for the weekly commercial meeting, and the dashboard archives every version so you can show ownership the trajectory over months.

STRENGTHS AND WEAKNESSES

What each competitor is being praised and criticised for, in plain English.

Expert tier adds a weekly Strengths and Weaknesses analysis per competitor. Claude AI reads all of their new reviews and produces a one-paragraph summary of what guests love about that hotel and what they complain about. You can read your five most-relevant competitors in two minutes.

The quarterly deep-dive briefing maps movement over time so you can see where a competitor is closing on you, where they are slipping, and where the market is shifting against the whole set. The briefing is written in narrative form — paragraphs, not bullet points — because the patterns that matter are usually structural, not transactional.

VERIFIED RATE COMPARISON

Rates with provenance, or hidden.

On Pro and Expert, the side-by-side includes verified rate comparison against your competitor set. Every rate displayed comes with source, timestamp, occupancy, currency, and a confidence status. Rates are hidden when data quality is not safe to display. We do not show speculative numbers dressed as facts.

Expert tier extends this into a room-rate market analysis: room-category match, demand-day pricing patterns, and parity violations across up to six competitors. The output is built to be useful in a revenue meeting — a defensible position your DOM can quote without disclaimers.

DAILY COMPETITOR MONITORING

Catch a competitor's bad week the day it starts.

Expert tier turns competitor monitoring into a daily stream. Every new review at every tracked competitor appears in your daily report, with sentiment and themes. If a rival hotel has a sudden cluster of cleanliness complaints, you see it the same morning their housekeeping manager does — sometimes earlier.

This is the level of intelligence that lets you move on a temporary opening in the market before the rival has time to recover. It is also the level that tells you when a peer property is genuinely outperforming you on a dimension where you used to lead — which is the harder conversation to have internally, but the more useful one.

START

Compare yourself to the hotels that matter.

Twenty-minute demo with a personalised competitor set during the call.

Book a demo

Related: Hotel reputation management · TripAdvisor monitoring · ReviewPro alternative · Pricing

faq.q: How many competitors can I track?
faq.a: Advanced tier tracks five competitors. Pro and Expert track ten. You can change the named set at any time.

faq.q: Where do you pull competitor reviews from?
faq.a: The same seven sources we use for your own property: Google, TripAdvisor, Booking.com, Expedia, Trip.com, ZenHotels and HolidayCheck.

faq.q: Are competitor rates included?
faq.a: Verified rate comparison is included on Pro and Expert (hotel only). Every rate carries source, timestamp, occupancy, currency and a confidence status, and rates are hidden when data quality is not safe to display.

faq.q: How often is competitor data refreshed?
faq.a: Daily on Advanced and Pro for the weekly side-by-side. Daily review-level monitoring is a feature of Expert tier.

faq.q: Can I pick competitors in different cities?
faq.a: Yes. The competitor set is whatever you name. Geography is up to you.

faq.q: What if a competitor's listing is hard to scrape?
faq.a: If we cannot reliably read a source for a given competitor, we will say so and we will not pretend to have data we do not have. You can always swap that competitor for another.

guest-feedback-analytics.title: Guest Feedback Analytics Software | ProInsights
guest-feedback-analytics.description: Guest feedback analytics for hotels and restaurants. AI-read reviews across Google, TripAdvisor and OTAs, with daily summaries, theme tracking and competitor benchmarks.
guest-feedback-analytics.h1: Guest feedback analytics that operators actually read
guest-feedback-analytics.intro: Most guest feedback analytics tools produce dashboards nobody opens. ProInsights produces a morning email and a WhatsApp message your GM reads with coffee — and a dashboard that backs it up when you want to dig deeper. Built for hotels and restaurants that want signal, not analytics theatre.
guest-feedback-analytics.body_html:
THE PROBLEM WITH DASHBOARDS

You did not buy a hotel to learn a BI tool.

The classic problem with guest feedback analytics is that the answer to "what did our guests say last week?" is buried three clicks deep in a dashboard nobody on the operations side has time to open. So nobody opens it. So nothing changes.

ProInsights inverts the model. The intelligence comes to you — in your inbox, on your phone — in a format you can read in two minutes. The dashboard is the archive for when you need to dig, not the daily workflow.

This is the single design decision that separates ProInsights from most reputation tools on the market. We accept that the dashboard is a secondary surface. The primary surface is an 8am email that fits on one phone screen, with a WhatsApp companion that delivers the headline in two lines.

WHAT WE ANALYSE

Every guest review, read by AI, sorted by what it tells you.

Sentiment

Every review scored as positive, mixed or negative — not just by overall stars but by theme. A review that praises the room and criticises breakfast registers both signals correctly.

Themes

Cleanliness, service, value, location, breakfast, noise, wait time, pace — auto-detected, ranked by frequency and movement over time.

Named entities

Staff members, dishes, room numbers, amenities — extracted by name and counted, so the language of your team and guests becomes structured data.

Comparative context

Every signal is set against the competitor benchmark — your themes versus their themes, your rating versus theirs, your trajectory versus the market.

Anomaly detection

Sudden clusters of complaints on one theme, or a spike of praise for a new dish, are flagged automatically in the daily brief so you do not have to look for them.

Long-form summaries

The monthly management report combines the structured data into a written narrative that ownership can read in five minutes.

DAILY, WEEKLY, MONTHLY

Three cadences, each tuned to its audience.

Daily report. What changed overnight, what to act on today. Aimed at the GM, the duty manager, the F&B head — the people running the floor.

Weekly report. Trend lines per theme, competitor side-by-side, top praised and top criticised items of the week. Aimed at the DOM, the F&B director, the head of housekeeping.

Monthly management report. A board-ready summary with rating movement, sentiment movement per department, and a written commentary. Aimed at ownership and the executive committee.

The same underlying data feeds all three. You do not have to choose between them — Advanced and above include weekly and monthly, so the whole team operates from the same source of truth at the cadence that suits them.

DASHBOARD AND EXPORT

A dashboard that supports the email, not the other way around.

The web dashboard archives every report you have ever received and lets you slice the underlying data: by source, by date range, by theme, by staff member, by competitor. Export to PDF for board packs. Filter by review type for a performance review. Pull thirty days of breakfast sentiment for the F&B director.

But none of this is required to get value from the product. Most users open the dashboard once a fortnight. The email and WhatsApp do the day-to-day work.

SOURCES

The seven sources we read, and only those seven.

For hotels: Google, TripAdvisor, Booking.com, Expedia, Trip.com, ZenHotels and HolidayCheck. For restaurants: Google and TripAdvisor. That is the full list. If a source matters to you and is not in that list, ask — we will tell you honestly whether we can add it or not. We do not pretend to monitor sources we cannot reliably read.

Honesty about scope is a deliberate stance. Plenty of platforms list dozens of "supported" sources where the coverage in practice is partial or stale. We would rather under-claim and deliver than the reverse.

START

Get the signal, skip the dashboard fatigue.

Twenty-minute demo with a personalised sample.

Book a demo

Related: Hotel reputation management · Restaurant review management · ReviewPro alternative · Pricing

faq.q: What is the difference between ProInsights and a generic NPS or survey tool?
faq.a: NPS and survey tools measure feedback you actively collect. ProInsights measures feedback your guests are already leaving in public — Google, TripAdvisor, the OTAs. They are complementary; we do not replace your post-stay survey.

faq.q: Can the report be customised per recipient?
faq.a: The body of the report is the same for everyone on the recipient list, so the team is aligned around one version of the truth. The WhatsApp summary on Pro is a condensed version of the same brief.

faq.q: How is sentiment measured?
faq.a: Every review is read by Claude AI and scored on overall sentiment plus sentiment per theme. The model is updated as the underlying language model evolves, so accuracy improves over time.

faq.q: Can I see historical trend lines?
faq.a: Yes. Weekly and monthly reports show trend lines by theme. The dashboard allows custom date-range queries down to the day for any theme or staff member you have tracked.

faq.q: Do you support hotels and restaurants on the same account?
faq.a: Yes. A group with multiple properties — including a mix of hotels and restaurants — gets each property on the right vertical, with multi-property discount on second and subsequent properties.

reviewpro-alternative.title: ReviewPro Alternative for Independent Hotels and Restaurants | ProInsights
reviewpro-alternative.description: A lighter, faster alternative to Shiji ReviewPro for independent hotels, boutique groups and restaurants. Daily WhatsApp and email intelligence — no enterprise rollout.
reviewpro-alternative.h1: A ReviewPro alternative for operators, not enterprises
reviewpro-alternative.intro: ReviewPro is a powerful platform with a long track record at large hotel groups. It is built around enterprise needs: central reputation teams, complex tagging hierarchies, integrations with CRS and PMS, multi-region rollouts. ProInsights is built differently — for the independent hotel, the boutique group, the restaurant operator who wants a daily brief on WhatsApp and email without the enterprise weight. This page lays out where each one fits.
reviewpro-alternative.body_html:
WHO EACH IS FOR

Two products, two operating models.

If you run a 200-property chain with a central reputation team in Madrid or Singapore, ReviewPro is probably the right answer. It is built around that operating model — dedicated analysts, deep integrations, a quarterly review cycle led by a reputation function.

If you run a single independent hotel, a small boutique group, or a busy restaurant, you do not have a central reputation team. You have a GM, an F&B director, and an owner who would like to know what changed overnight in the time it takes to drink a coffee. ProInsights is built around that operating model.

SIDE BY SIDE

Honest comparison.

FeatureProInsightsShiji ReviewPro
Built forIndependent hotels, boutique groups, restaurantsEnterprise hotel groups with central reputation teams
Daily deliveryEmail + WhatsApp morning summaryDashboard (email digest available)
Onboarding15-minute call, first report in 24hEnterprise rollout, typically weeks
Review sourcesGoogle, TripAdvisor, Booking, Expedia, Trip.com, ZenHotels, HolidayCheckBroad source coverage including channels we do not claim
AI reply draftsIncluded on Pro and aboveAvailable, varies by package
1-star alert30 minutes during operating hoursConfigurable alerting
Verified rate comparisonPro and Expert (with source, timestamp, occupancy, currency, confidence)Not the same product category
Competitor benchmarking5-10 named competitors, dailyAvailable, scaled to enterprise tagging
Pricing (hotel)€79 to €249 per property per monthEnterprise negotiated
ContractMonthly, cancel anytime, 30-day money-backAnnual enterprise contracts typical

This comparison is based on public information and our experience with both products. We do not claim ReviewPro lacks any specific capability — they are a serious team building a different product for a different buyer.

WHEN PROINSIGHTS IS THE RIGHT CHOICE

Five honest signals.

  • You are an independent hotel or a boutique group of one to ten properties
  • The GM and owner want to read the brief themselves — they are not going to delegate it to a reputation analyst
  • You want the morning summary on WhatsApp, on your phone, before service
  • You do not need integrations into central CRS, PMS or BI stacks
  • You want monthly billing, no setup fee, and the ability to cancel within thirty days

WHEN REVIEWPRO IS THE RIGHT CHOICE

Three honest signals.

  • You operate dozens of properties under a central reputation team
  • You need deep integration into a PMS, CRS or BI environment as a hard requirement
  • Your buying cycle is built around annual enterprise contracts and a procurement-led RFP

If any of those describe you, ReviewPro is a serious answer and we are happy to say so.

SWITCHING FROM REVIEWPRO

If you decide to move.

If you are coming off ReviewPro and switching to ProInsights, onboarding is the same as for any new customer — a 15-minute call to hand over your Google, TripAdvisor and OTA listings, name your competitors, and pick the tone of voice for AI replies. First real report in your inbox within 24 hours. You keep your ReviewPro account live until you have seen a few weeks of ProInsights output and decided to commit.

START

See ProInsights with your own data.

Twenty-minute demo. Personalised sample built during the call. No card needed.

Book a demo

Related: Hotel reputation management · Competitor benchmarking · Pricing

faq.q: Is ProInsights cheaper than ReviewPro?
faq.a: Almost certainly, yes — but the products are not exact substitutes. ProInsights is priced from €79 to €249 per property per month. ReviewPro is enterprise-negotiated and the two solve overlapping but distinct problems for different buyers.

faq.q: Do you integrate with PMS or CRS?
faq.a: Not currently. We focus on public review data — Google, TripAdvisor, Booking, Expedia, Trip.com, ZenHotels and HolidayCheck — and on getting the morning brief into your team's hands. If deep PMS or CRS integration is a hard requirement, ReviewPro is a better fit.

faq.q: What review sources do you cover that ReviewPro also covers?
faq.a: Google, TripAdvisor, Booking.com, Expedia, Trip.com, ZenHotels and HolidayCheck. ReviewPro covers additional sources we do not claim. We list ours explicitly and only those.

faq.q: Can a group with 30+ properties use ProInsights?
faq.a: Yes, technically — multi-property discount applies — but if the buying decision sits with a central reputation function and an enterprise procurement process, you should evaluate ReviewPro as well. We will tell you honestly when we think the other product is a better fit.

faq.q: Do you have a migration assistant for ReviewPro customers?
faq.a: Not as a productised offering, but onboarding for switchers is the same as for new customers — 15 minutes on a call, first real report within 24 hours, ReviewPro stays live until you decide to commit.

pricing.title: Pricing — Review Management Software for Hotels and Restaurants | ProInsights
pricing.description: Transparent pricing for ProInsights. Hotel: Basic €79, Advanced €139, Pro €189, Expert €249 per property per month. Restaurant and business tiers tailored.
pricing.h1: Transparent pricing. No demo required to see prices.
pricing.intro: Hotel pricing is published in full below. Restaurant and Business pricing is tailored to venue size and competitor scope — email us and we will quote within one working day. Multi-property: 20% off the second and subsequent properties. Monthly billing, no setup fee, cancel anytime, 30-day money-back if it is not earning its keep.
pricing.body_html:

Four tiers per property, per month.

Additional WhatsApp phones on Pro and Expert: €10 per phone per month. Multi-property: 20% off the second and subsequent properties (e.g. Pro €189 → €151).

RESTAURANT PRICING

Three tiers, tailored to your venue.

Restaurant pricing is not published as a list because the right number depends on your venue size, competitor scope, and which sources matter to you. The feature structure mirrors the hotel tiers, adjusted for restaurant reality — there is no Expert tier because Expert's headline feature is room-rate market analysis, which has no restaurant analogue.

TierWhat you get
BasicDaily report · Google Maps + TripAdvisor · Sentiment · Themes · 1 email recipient
AdvancedEverything in Basic · Multiple recipients · Weekly + Monthly reports · Competitor tracking · Dish + staff mentions
ProEverything in Advanced · WhatsApp morning summary · AI-drafted replies · 1-star instant alert

Get a quote

BUSINESS PRICING

For any business with a Google Business Profile.

Spas, dental clinics, lawyers, retail, gyms — any business that lives or dies by Google reviews. Pricing is tailored. The feature structure mirrors hotel and restaurant, scoped to Google Maps as the primary source.

TierWhat you get
BasicDaily report · Google Maps only · Sentiment · 1 email recipient
AdvancedEverything in Basic · Multiple recipients · Weekly + Monthly · Competitor tracking
ProEverything in Advanced · WhatsApp · AI replies · 1-star instant alert

Get a quote

STANDARD TERMS

The same for every tier and every vertical.

  • All tiers include everything below them within the same vertical
  • Data refreshed daily
  • Monthly billing — cancel anytime, no setup fee
  • First report within 24 hours of signature
  • 30-day money-back: if you are not getting value at thirty days, cancel and we refund the subscription, no questions, no exit fee
  • Multi-property: 20% off the second and subsequent properties

The monthly-billing-with-money-back posture is deliberate. We would rather you stay because the morning report is useful than because you are tied to a 24-month annual contract you signed in a sales cycle. If we cannot earn the keep month by month, you should not be paying us.

WHICH TIER FITS

A short guide to picking the right tier.

Basic (€79) is the right starting point for a small independent hotel that wants the daily report and theme detection on the six standard portals, with no competitor tracking yet. Most operators move off Basic within a few months as the value of competitor benchmarking becomes obvious.

Advanced (€139) adds five competitors tracked daily, plus the weekly and monthly reports for the wider management team. The €60 step up from Basic is the cheapest competitor-benchmark you will find in the European market and where most boutique hotels settle.

Pro (€189) is where most serious operators end up. WhatsApp morning summary, AI-drafted replies in your house voice, verified rate comparison, ten competitors, and the 90-minute one-star alert. Pro is the tier the platform is designed around.

Expert (€249) is for properties that need full daily competitor monitoring, room-rate market analysis with up to six competitors, and the weekly Strengths and Weaknesses analysis. Typically five-star and boutique properties competing in a tight, named peer set.

START

Pick the tier or ask for a quote.

Book a demo

Related: Hotel reputation management · Restaurant review management · ReviewPro alternative

faq.q: Why is restaurant pricing not published?
faq.a: Restaurant venues vary enormously in review volume and competitor scope. We would rather quote you a number we can stand behind than publish a list price that misrepresents what we will charge in practice. Email us and we will respond within one working day.

faq.q: Is there a setup fee?
faq.a: No. There is no setup fee on any tier or any vertical.

faq.q: How does the 30-day money-back work?
faq.a: If at 30 days you decide ProInsights is not earning its keep, we refund the subscription you have paid. No exit fee, no question, no friction. You keep the reports you have received.

faq.q: How does the multi-property discount work?
faq.a: Your first property is at list price. Each additional property is at 20% off the same tier — so Pro at €189 becomes €151 from your second property onwards.

faq.q: Can I mix tiers across properties?
faq.a: Yes. A group might run the flagship hotel on Expert and three sister properties on Pro. The 20% multi-property discount applies to each property at its own tier.

faq.q: Are there annual discounts?
faq.a: Not currently — we prefer monthly billing because it keeps both sides honest. If you want to discuss an annual arrangement for a multi-property group, get in touch.

faq.q: Do you charge per user?
faq.a: No. We charge per property, per month. Email recipients on Advanced are capped at five; on Pro and above they are unlimited. WhatsApp phones beyond the first on Pro and above are €10 per phone per month.

cyprus/restaurant-review-management.title: Restaurant Review Management Cyprus | ProInsights
cyprus/restaurant-review-management.description: Daily Google and TripAdvisor review intelligence for restaurants in Limassol, Nicosia, Larnaca, Paphos, Ayia Napa and Protaras. Built in Cyprus for Cypriot hospitality.
cyprus/restaurant-review-management.intro: Built in Cyprus for Cypriot restaurants. Daily Google and TripAdvisor intelligence for tavernas in Nicosia, beach-front venues in Ayia Napa and Protaras, fine-dining rooms in Limassol, harbour restaurants in Larnaca, and the seasonal mountain spots of Troodos. One short email every morning, optional WhatsApp brief, alerts within 90 minutes on any one-star review.
cyprus/restaurant-review-management.body_html:
BUILT IN CYPRUS

A platform made for the way Cyprus actually does hospitality.

Cypriot restaurants run on a seasonal rhythm — the summer months in Ayia Napa, Protaras and Paphos are a different operation from the winter trade in Nicosia and Limassol. Reviews swing accordingly. ProInsights is built in Cyprus, by a team who has worked in the local F&B industry, and it understands that rhythm.

The morning report works in English, Greek and Russian — the three languages your guests are most likely to leave reviews in. AI replies are drafted in the language of the review, so a Greek-speaking guest leaving a Greek-language review on Google gets a reply in Greek.

REGIONS WE COVER

Every region of Cyprus, restaurants of every size.

Limassol

From the Marina fine-dining rooms to the old-town tavernas, Limassol restaurants face the most international guest mix on the island — and the highest competitor density. ProInsights tracks ten named competitors daily on Pro.

Nicosia

A largely local market with year-round trade, Nicosia restaurants live or die by repeat custom and local Google reviews. The daily brief catches changes fast so you can address them at the next service.

Larnaca

Mixed local-and-tourist demand around the seafront and the salt lake. ProInsights handles the seasonal switch between TripAdvisor-heavy summer months and Google-heavy winter months without you having to retune anything.

Paphos

High-volume tourist trade with a long season. The 1-star alert is especially valuable here — catching a bad review at lunch versus the following morning often makes the difference for a venue running fifty covers a sitting.

Ayia Napa & Protaras

Seasonal volume that scales hard between May and October. ProInsights handles the volume swing without you having to upgrade or downgrade tier through the year — you pay for the season you are operating in.

Troodos and the villages

Lower review volume but higher stakes per review for small mountain tavernas. The dish leaderboard and named-staff mentions are typically more useful at this scale than the competitor tracking.

LANGUAGES

English, Greek, Russian — and the rest.

Cypriot restaurants get reviews in many languages. ProInsights reads and replies in English, Greek, Russian, French, German, Italian, Hebrew and Polish as standard, with other languages on request. The AI reply draft is always in the language of the original review, in your house voice.

A REAL EXAMPLE

Ararat — a working restaurant on ProInsights.

Ararat is a production Cypriot restaurant tenant on ProInsights. Daily reports, Google and TripAdvisor monitoring, named-dish and named-staff tracking, competitor benchmarking. We say this not as a marketing claim but as a fact — there is a live customer running in production today, and the platform's design has been shaped by that day-to-day use.

LOCAL SUPPORT

A team in your timezone and your language.

You can pick up the phone in Greek or English and talk to someone who knows the local industry. Onboarding calls are usually done in Greek for Cypriot customers. Account changes, tier upgrades and competitor swaps are handled by reply to the morning email — no support ticket system to navigate.

START

Add intelligence to your Cyprus restaurant.

Twenty-minute demo in Greek or English. Personalised sample for your venue during the call. First real report inside 24 hours of signature.

Book a demo

Related: Restaurant review management · Google review monitoring · Hotel reputation Cyprus · Pricing

faq.q: Do you have customers in Cyprus already?
faq.a: Yes. Ararat is a production Cypriot restaurant tenant on ProInsights, with daily reports running live. Other Cypriot venues are in pilot and onboarding.

faq.q: Can the morning report be in Greek?
faq.a: The morning report is currently written in English with the underlying reviews quoted in their original language. The AI reply drafts go out in the language of the review (including Greek). If your team prefers a Greek-language summary, tell us and we will quote a custom option.

faq.q: Will this work for a seasonal taverna in Ayia Napa?
faq.a: Yes. You can scale up to a higher tier through summer and back down through winter. Monthly billing, no contract trap, no setup fee on either change.

faq.q: How do you handle reviews in Russian and Hebrew?
faq.a: Russian and Hebrew reviews are read and analysed natively, and the AI draft reply is generated in the same language. Both are common on the south coast and we have tuned the model to handle them properly.

faq.q: How quickly will I get my first report?
faq.a: Within 24 hours of signature. Onboarding is a 15-minute call — typically in Greek for Cypriot customers — where you hand us your Google and TripAdvisor URLs and name your competitors.

faq.q: What does it cost for a Cyprus restaurant?
faq.a: Restaurant pricing is tailored. Most Cypriot restaurants land in the same range as our published hotel tiers (€79 to €189). Email hello@proinsights.com.cy and we will quote within one working day.

cyprus/hotel-reputation-management.title: Hotel Reputation Management Cyprus | ProInsights
cyprus/hotel-reputation-management.description: Daily hotel review intelligence for properties in Limassol, Paphos, Larnaca, Ayia Napa and Protaras. Multilingual replies, OTA monitoring, verified rate comparison.
cyprus/hotel-reputation-management.intro: Built in Cyprus for Cypriot hotels. Daily review intelligence across Google, TripAdvisor, Booking.com, Expedia, Trip.com, ZenHotels and HolidayCheck — the seven sources that actually move bookings for properties in Limassol, Paphos, Larnaca, Ayia Napa and Protaras. One morning email, optional WhatsApp summary, sentiment by department, verified rate comparison against your named competitor set.
cyprus/hotel-reputation-management.body_html:
BUILT IN CYPRUS

A platform tuned to the European OTA mix Cyprus actually sells through.

Cypriot hotels live on a different OTA mix to most of Europe. Booking.com is huge. Expedia is significant. Trip.com matters for the Asian market. HolidayCheck and ZenHotels punch above their weight for German, Polish and Eastern-European demand. ProInsights covers all of them as first-class sources — not as bolt-ons.

The team behind ProInsights is based in Cyprus and runs in your timezone. Onboarding calls can be in Greek or English. Account changes are by reply to the morning email — no ticket system, no off-island support team.

REGIONS WE COVER

From Limassol seafront to Paphos resorts.

Limassol

City-and-resort hybrid market with the highest international guest density on the island. Up to ten named competitors tracked daily on Pro, including the obvious five-star peer set.

Paphos

Long-season resort trade with deep Booking.com and Expedia volumes. The daily report catches the weekly rhythm of arrivals and departures so trends do not get lost in noise.

Larnaca

Airport-adjacent and seafront properties with mixed business-and-leisure demand. The verified rate comparison is especially useful for properties competing on weekend price.

Ayia Napa & Protaras

Highly seasonal beach properties. Pro and Expert tiers help you watch the competitor set tighten through July and August and the rate environment shift in real time.

Nicosia

The business-travel core of Cyprus. Lower review volume per property but high stakes per review. Sentiment by department surfaces F&B, meeting facilities and front-office issues quickly.

Troodos and rural Cyprus

Smaller properties with thinner review streams but distinct guest profiles. The dashboard archive matters more here for trend analysis over a long season.

VERIFIED RATE COMPARISON

Rates with provenance — or hidden.

Pro and Expert tiers include rate comparison against your named competitor set. Every rate displayed comes with source, timestamp, occupancy, currency, and a confidence status. Rates are hidden when data quality is not safe to display. We do not bluff numbers your revenue manager has to defend in a meeting.

Expert tier extends this into a full room-rate market analysis — up to six competitors, room-category match, demand-day pricing patterns, parity violations flagged automatically. The aim is intelligence your DOM can use, not a data dump that needs interpretation.

LANGUAGES

English, Greek, Russian, German, French — all native.

Cypriot hotels get reviews in many languages. ProInsights reads and analyses reviews natively in English, Greek, Russian, German, French, Italian, Polish, Dutch, Hebrew and Romanian. AI reply drafts on Pro and above are generated in the language the review was written in — a German guest gets a German reply in your house voice, a Russian guest a Russian one.

LOCAL CONTEXT

A team that knows the Cypriot operating reality.

Cypriot hotel operations have specifics that international platforms tend to flatten — the all-inclusive market dynamics, the Russian-language drop after 2022, the German tour-operator weight in shoulder season, the rapid spend on F&B in Limassol Marina-adjacent properties. ProInsights is built by people who know that context, and the support team you talk to has worked in or around the local industry.

START

Bring your hotel onto a daily brief.

Twenty-minute demo in Greek or English. Personalised sample for your property during the call. First real report inside 24 hours of signature. Cancel within 30 days for a full refund.

Book a demo

Related: Hotel reputation management · Competitor benchmarking · Restaurant reviews Cyprus · Pricing

faq.q: Which sources matter most for Cyprus hotels?
faq.a: Booking.com is the largest single source for most Cypriot hotels, with TripAdvisor and Google second and third depending on the property. Expedia matters for North American and UK demand, Trip.com for Asia, HolidayCheck and ZenHotels for the German and Eastern-European tour-operator markets. We cover all seven as first-class sources.

faq.q: Can I see verified competitor rates without signing up?
faq.a: We will show you sample rate comparisons during the demo, drawn from your actual competitor set on the day. Each rate carries source, timestamp, occupancy, currency and confidence — so you can see the verification trail before you commit.

faq.q: Can the morning report go to ownership in Athens or London?
faq.a: Yes. On Pro and Expert there is no recipient cap, so the GM, DOM, ownership and asset manager can all be on the same brief. The WhatsApp summary on Pro covers one phone — additional phones are €10 a month each.

faq.q: Is onboarding in Greek?
faq.a: If you prefer. The onboarding call is typically done in Greek for Cypriot customers and in English for international owners. Either is fine.

faq.q: Do you have Cypriot hotels using the platform already?
faq.a: We have Cypriot hotel deployments in production and in pilot. Specific names are available on request during the demo call — we do not list customer logos without explicit permission.

faq.q: How is this different from Shiji ReviewPro?
faq.a: ReviewPro is the right product for enterprise chains with central reputation teams. ProInsights is built for independent Cypriot hotels and boutique groups that want a daily WhatsApp and email brief without an enterprise rollout. See our ReviewPro alternative page for an honest side-by-side.
